description_long: This is a traditional water well featuring a thick, polygonal stone wall forming a circular basin. The well is topped with a sturdy wooden frame that supports a rope and pulley system for drawing water. A wooden bucket is attached to the rope, hanging over the well opening, and the rope is wound around a horizontal spindle at the top. The stone surface appears weathered and slightly rough, while the wood has a natural, unfinished look. The well is open at the top, with no visible cover, allowing direct access to the water below. The structure is robust and utilitarian, designed for durability and frequent use in an outdoor urban or rural setting.
description: A stone water well with a wooden frame, rope, and bucket, featuring a weathered, open design. The well is robust and utilitarian, with visible stone and wood textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden frame, rope, and bucket hanging over the opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone wall and the wooden frame's vertical support.
description_view_2: Rear view with the bucket more visible to the right and the rope extending into the well.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden frame and the bucket resting near the edge of the well.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A rope and bucket mechanism is present, suspended from a wooden frame with a spindle at the top.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a thick stone wall approximately waist-high, serving as a protective barrier.
